templates.title: Safe Pickle — load/dump dengan verifikasi hash
templates.description: Pickle dengan hash SHA256 anti-tamper. Serialize + integrity check.
templates.code:
def {{nama_fungsi_dump}}(data, filepath):
    """Pickle data to file with SHA256 integrity hash.
    File format: [4 bytes hash length][hash bytes][pickled data]
    """
    pickled = pickle.dumps(data, protocol=pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL)
    digest = hashlib.sha256(pickled).digest()
    hash_len = len(digest).to_bytes(4, 'big')
    with open(filepath, 'wb') as f:
        f.write(hash_len)
        f.write(digest)
        f.write(pickled)


def {{nama_fungsi_load}}(filepath):
    """Load pickled data with integrity verification.
    Raises ValueError if file is tampered or corrupted.
    """
    with open(filepath, 'rb') as f:
        hash_len_bytes = f.read(4)
        if len(hash_len_bytes) < 4:
            raise ValueError('File corrupted: missing hash length')
        hash_len = int.from_bytes(hash_len_bytes, 'big')
        stored_hash = f.read(hash_len)
        pickled = f.read()
    computed_hash = hashlib.sha256(pickled).digest()
    if computed_hash != stored_hash:
        raise ValueError('Pickle integrity check failed — file may be tampered')
    return pickle.loads(pickled)

templates.title: Binary Struct Reader
templates.description: Baca file binary dengan struct format string. Parse header, fields, arrays.
templates.code:
class {{nama_class}}:
    """Read binary files using struct format strings.
    Format codes: 'i'=int32, 'I'=uint32, 'h'=int16, 'f'=float32, 'd'=float64,
                  'B'=uint8, 's'=char, etc. Prefix with '<' for little-endian, '>' for big-endian.
    """
    def __init__(self, filepath, default_endian='<'):
        self.filepath = filepath
        self.default_endian = default_endian

    def read_struct(self, fmt, offset=0):
        """Read struct at offset. fmt like '<3i2f' (3 ints, 2 floats)."""
        full_fmt = fmt if fmt[0] in '<>@!' else self.default_endian + fmt
        size = struct.calcsize(full_fmt)
        with open(self.filepath, 'rb') as f:
            f.seek(offset)
            data = f.read(size)
        if len(data) < size:
            raise ValueError('Not enough data: expected ' + str(size) + ' bytes, got ' + str(len(data)))
        return struct.unpack(full_fmt, data)

    def read_array(self, item_fmt, count, offset=0):
        """Read array of count items."""
        full_fmt = item_fmt if item_fmt[0] in '<>@!' else self.default_endian + item_fmt
        item_size = struct.calcsize(full_fmt)
        results = []
        with open(self.filepath, 'rb') as f:
            f.seek(offset)
            for _ in range(count):
                data = f.read(item_size)
                if len(data) < item_size:
                    break
                results.append(struct.unpack(full_fmt, data))
        return results

    def read_string(self, offset, length):
        """Read fixed-length string at offset."""
        with open(self.filepath, 'rb') as f:
            f.seek(offset)
            data = f.read(length)
        return data.rstrip(b'\x00').decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

    def file_size(self):
        """Get file size in bytes."""
        import os
        return os.path.getsize(self.filepath)

templates.title: CSV Dialect Auto-Detector
templates.description: Deteksi delimiter, quotechar, dan dialect CSV secara otomatis dari sample baris.
templates.code:
def {{nama_fungsi}}(filepath_or_text, sample_lines={{sample_lines}}):
    """Auto-detect CSV dialect (delimiter, quotechar, etc).
    Accepts file path or raw text string.
    Returns dict with dialect info: delimiter, quotechar, has_header, sample.
    """
    if hasattr(filepath_or_text, 'read'):
        text = filepath_or_text.read()
    elif isinstance(filepath_or_text, str) and '\n' in filepath_or_text:
        text = filepath_or_text
    else:
        with open(filepath_or_text, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            text = f.read()
    lines = text.splitlines()[:sample_lines]
    sample = '\n'.join(lines)
    try:
        dialect = csv.Sniffer().sniff(sample, delimiters=',;\t|')
        has_header = csv.Sniffer().has_header(sample)
    except csv.Error:
        dialect = csv.excel
        has_header = True
    return {
        'delimiter': dialect.delimiter,
        'quotechar': dialect.quotechar,
        'doublequote': dialect.doublequote,
        'skipinitialspace': dialect.skipinitialspace,
        'has_header': has_header,
        'sample': lines[0] if lines else '',
        'column_count': len(lines[0].split(dialect.delimiter)) if lines else 0,
    }
